title: Plat thinpak vs Platinum
wikitext:
[]: Plat thinpak vs. Platinum             Quote Post by narcosis219 » Wed Aug 02, 2006 7:51 pm Is there a difference between the Platinum thinpak and the individual Platinum boxes? I'm planning to get a set, and im wondering which ones to buy.

[]:             Quote Post by Shadow Blaziken » Wed Aug 02, 2006 7:52 pm I heard that buying the thinpak is cheaper than buying all the DVDs separate. So the thinpak is a bit less expensive.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Wed Aug 02, 2006 7:54 pm Thinpak wins.

[]:             Quote Post by narcosis219 » Wed Aug 02, 2006 7:54 pm I'm aware that the thinpak is cheaper, but i'm wondering if what im missing in the original platiums that the thinpak doesnt have is worth it or not

[]:             Quote Post by ZeroFive1 » Wed Aug 02, 2006 7:55 pm It doesn't make that much of a difference, unless you're a super eva fan. Besides, you save about 20 bucks.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Aug 02, 2006 8:02 pm And here I spent all this time writing a review of the thinpak... and it's purpose went unfullfilled. http://www.evamonkey.com/reviews_platinum_thinpak.php

[]:             Quote Post by Timstuff » Thu Aug 03, 2006 12:47 am ZeroFive1 wrote: It doesn't make that much of a difference, unless you're a super eva fan. Besides, you save about 20 bucks.

[]:             Quote Post by drinian » Thu Aug 03, 2006 8:37 am ADV thinpaks, in general, don't have any of the extra features of the original releases (as noted in Aaron's review). So it'rs really up to whether you want to watch anything besides the series itself. Also, isn't the regular Platinum set out of print now, in anticipation of the 10th Anniversiary set?

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Aug 03, 2006 2:18 pm drinian wrote: Also, isn't the regular Platinum set out of print now, in anticipation of the 10th Anniversiary set?
